name: Crawfish and Corn Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Corn - cut from the cob, reserving about 4 cobs
1 bunch Green onions - finely chopped (use about 2 or 3 inches of the fresh green part)
3 cloves Garlic - minced (3 or 4 cloves, depending on size)
recipeInstructions:
Number of Servings: 8
1. Make the stock first. Ten pounds of boiled crawfish yield one pound of meat. Peel the crawfish, reserving the shells and claws. Rinse the shells and let drain.
2. Melt butter in a large pot; add the shells and fry until sizzling and hot, turning them often.
3. When the shells are hot enough, ignite the brandy in a small pot. Remove the shells from heat and pour in the brandy, tossing and turning until the flame dies down.
4. Add wine and water to cover the shells. Add onion, celery, green onions, parsley, garlic, thyme, and bay leaf. Let it all simmer for an hour.
5. Strain carefully and reserve the stock.
6. In another pot, melt butter, add flour and cook on low heat until medium brown.
7. Add chopped vegetables and cook on low heat until wilted, stirring often.
8. Chop tomatoes and add to the pot with the reserved liquid. Let cook a few minutes.
9. Add the stock - just enough to make a soupy consistency. Then add Worcestershire sauce, bay leaf, thyme, crawfish, corn, and about four of the cobs (for added flavor). Let simmer for an hour.
10. Taste for salt, pepper, and Tabasco. If using boiled crawfish, it is usually peppery enough.
11. Remove the cobs and serve. If the soup is too thick, add more stock or water. If it's too thin, cook a little more flour and add to thicken it.
